channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Has not been cleaned for at least 20 years., 3rd March
item.description:
This problem has been going on constantly for sat least 20 yesrs although reprsted reports

item.title: New pavement. Pink in sea of grey, 3rd March

item.title: Mattress fly tipping hot spot, 3rd March
item.description:
Another mattress dumped on the corner of Ceres road in what has become a weekly issue

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kashgar Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole / blocked drain, 3rd March
item.description:
Large pothole combined with a blocked drain at the traffic light junction between Halfway Street & Hurst Road, outside of Holy Trinity Church. The pothole is growing ever larger. Every time it rains the drain backs up making the pothole even bigger and invisible to cyclists and other road users, creating a very dangerous scenario. Pooling water is then splashed by passing vehicles onto pedestrians on the footpath running past the church
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 161, Station Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Santander bike- been here fora while

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kemnal Road, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: At junction with Kentish Town Road, 3rd March
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Hawley Crescent, Chalk Farm, Greater London

item.title: The cyclist traffic signal is turned, 3rd March
item.description:
Cannot see the traffic light when heading southbound as its been turned
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Great Eastern Street, Shoreditch, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Damage - pole leaning / equipment detached from pole/facing the wrong direction

item.title: Rubbish everywhere in entrance and within locked carpark area, pathway blocked, 3rd March
item.description:
Rubbish everywhere in entrance and within locked carpark area, pathway blocked with dumped garden waste and rubbish, bottles in hedgerows and within thickets from magpie hall lane entrance at astley building to holbrook way entrance. This has been there for over a year and reported previously but the council never attends and cleans up, it's a mess and needs a proper visit and clean up.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Magpie Hall Lane, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
